{{wikidata link|Q11646210}} == Japanese content == {{神社 | 名称=Noma Shrine | 画像=[[File:今治野間神社2.jpg|240px|Torii]]
Torii of Noma Shrine（{{ill|lt=2025|Q49616|WD=Q49616|1=2025}}{{ill|lt=December|Q126|WD=Q126|1=December}}） | 所在地={{ill|lt=Ehime Prefecture|Q123376|WD=Q123376|1=Ehime Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Imabari|Q461020|WD=Q461020|1=Imabari, Ehime}}Kannomiya-chō 699 | 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|3|3.6|N|132|57|25.7|E|region:JP-38_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Noma Shrine}} | 祭神=Ayahayatama-no-Mikoto
Wakayao-no-Mikoto
{{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}
Noma-hime-no-Mikoto | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}（{{ill|lt=myōjin taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|lt=myōjin-tai|1=Myōjin Taisha}}）・{{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=Ken-sha}} | 創建=Unknown | 本殿={{ill|lt=irimoya-zukuri|Q855025|WD=Q855025|1=East Asian hip-and-gable roof}} | 例祭={{ill|lt=May 3|Q2547|WD=Q2547|1=May 3}} | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Ehime|label=Noma Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Noma Shrine'''（のまじんじゃ）is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Kannomiya, {{ill|lt=Imabari|Q461020|WD=Q461020|1=Imabari, Ehime}}, {{ill|lt=Ehime Prefecture|Q123376|WD=Q123376|1=Ehime Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}（{{ill|lt=myōjin taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|lt=myōjin-tai|1=Myōjin Taisha}}）, and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=Ken-sha}}. === Enshrined deities === *{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ayahayatama-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} *{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Wakayao-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} *{{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} *{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Noma-hime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} There are multiple theories about the enshrined deities, but these four are currently treated as the shrine's deities. Ayahayatama-no-Mikoto is the deity of {{ill|lt=Hayatama Shrine|Q11639721|WD=Q11639721|1=Hayatani Shrine}} and ancestor of the {{ill|lt=Aki-no-kuni-no-miyatsuko|Q21654103|WD=Q21654103|lt=provincial governors of Aki|1=provincial governors of Aki}}. Wakayao-no-Mikoto is his third-generation descendant and ancestor of the {{ill|lt=Nukata-no-kuni-no-miyatsuko|Q21654103|WD=Q21654103|1=Nukata-no-kuni-no-miyatsuko}}. Noma-hime-no-Mikoto is regarded as Wakayao-no-Mikoto's wife. Because Noma Shrine was called "Noma Tennō-gami" and "Nōman Tennō-gami," there is also a theory that its deity was {{ill|lt=Gozu Tennō|Q11570247|WD=Q11570247|1=Gozu Tennō}}, with {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} as principal deity. However, ''Noma Jinja-shi'' considers Ayahayatama-no-Mikoto, Wakayao-no-Mikoto, and Noma-hime-no-Mikoto to be the main deities, and Susanoo-no-Mikoto to have been an associated deity. That there was a medieval period when {{ill|lt=Gozu Tennō|Q11570247|WD=Q11570247|1=Gozu Tennō}} was enshrined is clear from documents remaining at the temple-bettō {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Jōzen-ji|1=UNKNOWN}}. === Origins === Its founding is unknown, but it is believed to have begun when Wakayao-no-Mikoto, who became {{ill|lt=Nukata-no-kuni-no-miyatsuko|Q21654103|WD=Q21654103|1=Nukata-no-kuni-no-miyatsuko}}, worshiped his ancestor {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ayahayatama-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}. In the ''{{ill|lt=Kuni no miyatsuko hongi|Q2716751|WD=Q2716751|lt=Sendai Kuji Hongi: Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi|1=Kujiki}}'', Wakayao-no-Mikoto is said to have been appointed provincial governor in the reign of {{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|WD=Q232803|1=Empress Jingū}}. According to shrine tradition, a shrine building was constructed in {{ill|lt=701|Q23496|WD=Q23496|1=701}}（{{ill|lt=Taihō|Q1346269|WD=Q1346269|lt=Taihō|1=Taihō (era)}} 1）. In {{ill|lt=766|Q30564|WD=Q30564|1=766}}（{{ill|lt=Tenpyō-jingo|Q1373181|WD=Q1373181|1=Tenpyō-jingo}} 2）, it was granted Junior Fifth Rank, Lower and given two households of shrine serfs. In {{ill|lt=837|Q25093|WD=Q25093|1=837}}（{{ill|lt=Jōwa|Q1075605|WD=Q1075605|lt=Jōwa|1=Jōwa (Heian period)}} 4）, together with {{ill|lt=Ōyamazumi Shrine|Q703904|WD=Q703904|1=Ōyamazumi Shrine}}, it received myōjin status for the first time in the {{ill|lt=Nankaidō|Q1376747|WD=Q1376747|1=Nankaidō}} region. In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}, it is listed as a {{ill|lt=myōjin taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}}, and in {{ill|lt=940|Q23830|WD=Q23830|1=940}}（{{ill|lt=Tengyō|Q1327340|WD=Q1327340|1=Tengyō}} 3）, due to prayers against pirate raiding, it was promoted to Senior Second Rank. In the medieval period it was strongly revered by military leaders, especially the {{ill|lt=Kōno clan|Q11554232|WD=Q11554232|1=Kōno clan}}. In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, it served as a {{ill|lt=rainmaking|Q477738|WD=Q477738|lt=rain-prayer site|1=Rainmaking (ritual)}} for the {{ill|lt=Matsuyama Domain|Q1042087|WD=Q1042087|lt=Matsuyama domain of Iyo|1=Iyo-Matsuyama Domain}}. In 1871（Meiji 4）it was ranked {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}, and in 1895（Meiji 28）it was promoted to {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=Ken-sha}}. === Shrine name === In sources such as the {{ill|lt=Shoku Nihongi|Q611399|WD=Q611399|1=Shoku Nihongi}}, it appears as "Noma-no-kami"; in the {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q1052981|lt=Sandai Jitsuroku|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}, as "Noma Tennō-gami"; and in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, as "Noma Shrine." The writing of "Noma" also included characters such as 乃万, 濃満, and 濃万, deriving from {{ill|lt=Noma District|Q266830|WD=Q266830|1=Noma District, Ehime}}（{{ill|lt=Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi|Q133833194|WD=Q133833194|1=Kokuzo Hongi}}'s {{ill|lt=Nukata Province|Q21654103|WD=Q21654103|lt=Nukata-no-kuni|1=Nukata-no-kuni}}）. In the Kenchō-era documents it appears as "Nomangu," and in Kōfuku-ji's solicited-deity register as "Shōichii Nōman Tennō-gami." There are also examples such as "Daibonten'ō-gū" and "Nomasha Daibonten'ō-gū." Among local people it was called "Tennō-sha" or "Tennō-gū," and popularly known as "Tennon-san." 今治野間神社.jpg|Sanmon（{{ill|lt=2025|Q49616|WD=Q49616|1=2025}}{{ill|lt=December|Q126|WD=Q126|1=December}}） 今治野間神社4.jpg|Hokora（December 2025） 今治野間神社5.jpg|Honden（December 2025） 今治野間神社6.jpg|Stone steps（December 2025） === Cultural properties === ;Important Cultural Property *Stone {{ill|lt=Hōkyōintō|Q3146365|WD=Q3146365|1=Hōkyōintō}} built in {{ill|lt=1322|Q5770|WD=Q5770|1=1322}}（{{ill|lt=Genkō|Q509889|WD=Q509889|1=Genkō (1321–1324)}} 2） ;Prefectural Tangible Cultural Property *11 {{ill|lt=Japanese bronze mirrors|Q1041984|WD=Q1041984|lt=wa-kyō|1=Bronze mirror}} ;Prefectural Intangible Folk Cultural Property *{{ill|lt=Shishimai|Q713866|WD=Q713866|1=Lion dance}} transmitted at Kannomiya Noma Shrine 今治野間神社3.jpg|Important Cultural Property stone {{ill|lt=Hōkyōintō|Q3146365|WD=Q3146365|1=Hōkyōintō}} behind the honden（December 2025） === Annual festival === At the annual grand festival held on May 3, dedicatory performances include the Urayasu dance, daimyo procession, lion dance, mikoshi procession, and hemp-plain sacred boat. Among these, the {{ill|lt=lion dance|Q713866|WD=Q713866|1=Lion dance}} is designated an {{ill|lt=Ehime Prefecture|Q123376|WD=Q123376|1=Ehime Prefecture}} intangible folk cultural property, and the mikoshi procession is designated an {{ill|lt=Imabari|Q461020|WD=Q461020|1=Imabari, Ehime}} intangible folk cultural property. === References === * ''Noma Jinja-shi'', Noma Jinja-shi Editorial Committee（Noma Shrine, 2007） === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Noma Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Noma Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Iyo Province]] [[Category:愛媛県の神社]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:伊予国の式内社|名のましんしや]] [[Category:愛媛県の旧県社]] [[Category:今治市の建築物]] [[Category:今治市の歴史]] [[Category:重要文化財 (建造物)]] [[Category:愛媛県の重要文化財]] [[Category:愛媛県指定有形文化財]] [[Category:都道府県指定無形民俗文化財]][[Category:qq]]

{{wikidata link|Q11646210}} == Japanese content == {{神社 | 名称=Noma Shrine | 画像=[[File:今治野間神社2.jpg|240px|Torii]]
Torii of Noma Shrine（{{ill|lt=2025|Q49616|WD=Q49616|1=2025}}{{ill|lt=December|Q126|WD=Q126|1=December}}） | 所在地={{ill|lt=Ehime Prefecture|Q123376|WD=Q123376|1=Ehime Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Imabari|Q461020|WD=Q461020|1=Imabari, Ehime}}Kannomiya-chō 699 | 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|3|3.6|N|132|57|25.7|E|region:JP-38_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Noma Shrine}} | 祭神=Ayahayatama-no-Mikoto
Wakayao-no-Mikoto
{{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}
Noma-hime-no-Mikoto | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}（{{ill|lt=myōjin taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|lt=myōjin-tai|1=Myōjin Taisha}}）・{{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=Ken-sha}} | 創建=Unknown | 本殿={{ill|lt=irimoya-zukuri|Q855025|WD=Q855025|1=East Asian hip-and-gable roof}} | 例祭={{ill|lt=May 3|Q2547|WD=Q2547|1=May 3}} | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Ehime|label=Noma Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Noma Shrine'''（のまじんじゃ）is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Kannomiya, {{ill|lt=Imabari|Q461020|WD=Q461020|1=Imabari, Ehime}}, {{ill|lt=Ehime Prefecture|Q123376|WD=Q123376|1=Ehime Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}（{{ill|lt=myōjin taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|lt=myōjin-tai|1=Myōjin Taisha}}）, and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=Ken-sha}}. === Enshrined deities === *{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ayahayatama-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} *{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Wakayao-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} *{{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} *{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Noma-hime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} There are multiple theories about the enshrined deities, but these four are currently treated as the shrine's deities. Ayahayatama-no-Mikoto is the deity of {{ill|lt=Hayatama Shrine|Q11639721|WD=Q11639721|1=Hayatani Shrine}} and ancestor of the {{ill|lt=Aki-no-kuni-no-miyatsuko|Q21654103|WD=Q21654103|lt=provincial governors of Aki|1=provincial governors of Aki}}. Wakayao-no-Mikoto is his third-generation descendant and ancestor of the {{ill|lt=Nukata-no-kuni-no-miyatsuko|Q21654103|WD=Q21654103|1=Nukata-no-kuni-no-miyatsuko}}. Noma-hime-no-Mikoto is regarded as Wakayao-no-Mikoto's wife. Because Noma Shrine was called "Noma Tennō-gami" and "Nōman Tennō-gami," there is also a theory that its deity was {{ill|lt=Gozu Tennō|Q11570247|WD=Q11570247|1=Gozu Tennō}}, with {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} as principal deity. However, ''Noma Jinja-shi'' considers Ayahayatama-no-Mikoto, Wakayao-no-Mikoto, and Noma-hime-no-Mikoto to be the main deities, and Susanoo-no-Mikoto to have been an associated deity. That there was a medieval period when {{ill|lt=Gozu Tennō|Q11570247|WD=Q11570247|1=Gozu Tennō}} was enshrined is clear from documents remaining at the temple-bettō {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Jōzen-ji|1=UNKNOWN}}. === Origins === Its founding is unknown, but it is believed to have begun when Wakayao-no-Mikoto, who became {{ill|lt=Nukata-no-kuni-no-miyatsuko|Q21654103|WD=Q21654103|1=Nukata-no-kuni-no-miyatsuko}}, worshiped his ancestor {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ayahayatama-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}. In the ''{{ill|lt=Kuni no miyatsuko hongi|Q2716751|WD=Q2716751|lt=Sendai Kuji Hongi: Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi|1=Kujiki}}'', Wakayao-no-Mikoto is said to have been appointed provincial governor in the reign of {{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|WD=Q232803|1=Empress Jingū}}. According to shrine tradition, a shrine building was constructed in {{ill|lt=701|Q23496|WD=Q23496|1=701}}（{{ill|lt=Taihō|Q1346269|WD=Q1346269|lt=Taihō|1=Taihō (era)}} 1）. In {{ill|lt=766|Q30564|WD=Q30564|1=766}}（{{ill|lt=Tenpyō-jingo|Q1373181|WD=Q1373181|1=Tenpyō-jingo}} 2）, it was granted Junior Fifth Rank, Lower and given two households of shrine serfs. In {{ill|lt=837|Q25093|WD=Q25093|1=837}}（{{ill|lt=Jōwa|Q1075605|WD=Q1075605|lt=Jōwa|1=Jōwa (Heian period)}} 4）, together with {{ill|lt=Ōyamazumi Shrine|Q703904|WD=Q703904|1=Ōyamazumi Shrine}}, it received myōjin status for the first time in the {{ill|lt=Nankaidō|Q1376747|WD=Q1376747|1=Nankaidō}} region. In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}, it is listed as a {{ill|lt=myōjin taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}}, and in {{ill|lt=940|Q23830|WD=Q23830|1=940}}（{{ill|lt=Tengyō|Q1327340|WD=Q1327340|1=Tengyō}} 3）, due to prayers against pirate raiding, it was promoted to Senior Second Rank. In the medieval period it was strongly revered by military leaders, especially the {{ill|lt=Kōno clan|Q11554232|WD=Q11554232|1=Kōno clan}}. In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, it served as a {{ill|lt=rainmaking|Q477738|WD=Q477738|lt=rain-prayer site|1=Rainmaking (ritual)}} for the {{ill|lt=Matsuyama Domain|Q1042087|WD=Q1042087|lt=Matsuyama domain of Iyo|1=Iyo-Matsuyama Domain}}. In 1871（Meiji 4）it was ranked {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}, and in 1895（Meiji 28）it was promoted to {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=Ken-sha}}. === Shrine name === In sources such as the {{ill|lt=Shoku Nihongi|Q611399|WD=Q611399|1=Shoku Nihongi}}, it appears as "Noma-no-kami"; in the {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q1052981|lt=Sandai Jitsuroku|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}, as "Noma Tennō-gami"; and in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, as "Noma Shrine." The writing of "Noma" also included characters such as 乃万, 濃満, and 濃万, deriving from {{ill|lt=Noma District|Q266830|WD=Q266830|1=Noma District, Ehime}}（{{ill|lt=Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi|Q133833194|WD=Q133833194|1=Kokuzo Hongi}}'s {{ill|lt=Nukata Province|Q21654103|WD=Q21654103|lt=Nukata-no-kuni|1=Nukata-no-kuni}}）. In the Kenchō-era documents it appears as "Nomangu," and in Kōfuku-ji's solicited-deity register as "Shōichii Nōman Tennō-gami." There are also examples such as "Daibonten'ō-gū" and "Nomasha Daibonten'ō-gū." Among local people it was called "Tennō-sha" or "Tennō-gū," and popularly known as "Tennon-san." 今治野間神社.jpg|Sanmon（{{ill|lt=2025|Q49616|WD=Q49616|1=2025}}{{ill|lt=December|Q126|WD=Q126|1=December}}） 今治野間神社4.jpg|Hokora（December 2025） 今治野間神社5.jpg|Honden（December 2025） 今治野間神社6.jpg|Stone steps（December 2025） === Cultural properties === ;Important Cultural Property *Stone {{ill|lt=Hōkyōintō|Q3146365|WD=Q3146365|1=Hōkyōintō}} built in {{ill|lt=1322|Q5770|WD=Q5770|1=1322}}（{{ill|lt=Genkō|Q509889|WD=Q509889|1=Genkō (1321–1324)}} 2） ;Prefectural Tangible Cultural Property *11 {{ill|lt=Japanese bronze mirrors|Q1041984|WD=Q1041984|lt=wa-kyō|1=Bronze mirror}} ;Prefectural Intangible Folk Cultural Property *{{ill|lt=Shishimai|Q713866|WD=Q713866|1=Lion dance}} transmitted at Kannomiya Noma Shrine 今治野間神社3.jpg|Important Cultural Property stone {{ill|lt=Hōkyōintō|Q3146365|WD=Q3146365|1=Hōkyōintō}} behind the honden（December 2025） === Annual festival === At the annual grand festival held on May 3, dedicatory performances include the Urayasu dance, daimyo procession, lion dance, mikoshi procession, and hemp-plain sacred boat. Among these, the {{ill|lt=lion dance|Q713866|WD=Q713866|1=Lion dance}} is designated an {{ill|lt=Ehime Prefecture|Q123376|WD=Q123376|1=Ehime Prefecture}} intangible folk cultural property, and the mikoshi procession is designated an {{ill|lt=Imabari|Q461020|WD=Q461020|1=Imabari, Ehime}} intangible folk cultural property. === References === * ''Noma Jinja-shi'', Noma Jinja-shi Editorial Committee（Noma Shrine, 2007） === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Noma Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Noma Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Iyo Province]] [[Category:愛媛県の神社]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:伊予国の式内社|名のましんしや]] [[Category:愛媛県の旧県社]] [[Category:今治市の建築物]] [[Category:History of Imabari, Ehime]] [[Category:重要文化財 (建造物)]] [[Category:愛媛県の重要文化財]] [[Category:愛媛県指定有形文化財]] [[Category:都道府県指定無形民俗文化財]][[Category:qq]]

In {{ill|lt=837|Q25093|WD=Q25093|1=837}}（{{ill|lt=Jōwa|Q1075605|WD=Q1075605|lt=Jōwa|1=Jōwa (Heian period)}} 4）, together with {{ill|lt=Ōyamazumi Shrine|Q703904|WD=Q703904|1=Ōyamazumi Shrine}}, it received myōjin status for the first time in the {{ill|lt=Nankaidō|Q1376747|WD=Q1376747|1=Nankaidō}} region. In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}, it is listed as a {{ill|lt=myōjin taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}}, and in {{ill|lt=940|Q23830|WD=Q23830|1=940}}（{{ill|lt=Tengyō|Q1327340|WD=Q1327340|1=Tengyō}} 3）, due to prayers against pirate raiding, it was promoted to Senior Second Rank. In the medieval period it was strongly revered by military leaders, especially the {{ill|lt=Kōno clan|Q11554232|WD=Q11554232|1=Kōno clan}}. In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, it served as a {{ill|lt=rainmaking|Q477738|WD=Q477738|lt=rain-prayer site|1=Rainmaking (ritual)}} for the {{ill|lt=Matsuyama Domain|Q1042087|WD=Q1042087|lt=Matsuyama domain of Iyo|1=Iyo-Matsuyama Domain}}. In 1871（Meiji 4）it was ranked {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}, and in 1895（Meiji 28）it was promoted to {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=Ken-sha}}. === Shrine name === In sources such as the {{ill|lt=Shoku Nihongi|Q611399|WD=Q611399|1=Shoku Nihongi}}, it appears as "Noma-no-kami"; in the {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q1052981|lt=Sandai Jitsuroku|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}, as "Noma Tennō-gami"; and in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, as "Noma Shrine." The writing of "Noma" also included characters such as 乃万, 濃満, and 濃万, deriving from {{ill|lt=Noma District|Q266830|WD=Q266830|1=Noma District, Ehime}}（{{ill|lt=Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi|Q133833194|WD=Q133833194|1=Kokuzo Hongi}}'s {{ill|lt=Nukata Province|Q21654103|WD=Q21654103|lt=Nukata-no-kuni|1=Nukata-no-kuni}}）. In the Kenchō-era documents it appears as "Nomangu," and in Kōfuku-ji's solicited-deity register as "Shōichii Nōman Tennō-gami." There are also examples such as "Daibonten'ō-gū" and "Nomasha Daibonten'ō-gū." Among local people it was called "Tennō-sha" or "Tennō-gū," and popularly known as "Tennon-san." 今治野間神社.jpg|Sanmon（{{ill|lt=2025|Q49616|WD=Q49616|1=2025}}{{ill|lt=December|Q126|WD=Q126|1=December}}） 今治野間神社4.jpg|Hokora（December 2025） 今治野間神社5.jpg|Honden（December 2025） 今治野間神社6.jpg|Stone steps（December 2025） === Cultural properties === ;Important Cultural Property *Stone {{ill|lt=Hōkyōintō|Q3146365|WD=Q3146365|1=Hōkyōintō}} built in {{ill|lt=1322|Q5770|WD=Q5770|1=1322}}（{{ill|lt=Genkō|Q509889|WD=Q509889|1=Genkō (1321–1324)}} 2） ;Prefectural Tangible Cultural Property *11 {{ill|lt=Japanese bronze mirrors|Q1041984|WD=Q1041984|lt=wa-kyō|1=Bronze mirror}} ;Prefectural Intangible Folk Cultural Property *{{ill|lt=Shishimai|Q713866|WD=Q713866|1=Lion dance}} transmitted at Kannomiya Noma Shrine 今治野間神社3.jpg|Important Cultural Property stone {{ill|lt=Hōkyōintō|Q3146365|WD=Q3146365|1=Hōkyōintō}} behind the honden（December 2025） === Annual festival === At the annual grand festival held on May 3, dedicatory performances include the Urayasu dance, daimyo procession, lion dance, mikoshi procession, and hemp-plain sacred boat. Among these, the {{ill|lt=lion dance|Q713866|WD=Q713866|1=Lion dance}} is designated an {{ill|lt=Ehime Prefecture|Q123376|WD=Q123376|1=Ehime Prefecture}} intangible folk cultural property, and the mikoshi procession is designated an {{ill|lt=Imabari|Q461020|WD=Q461020|1=Imabari, Ehime}} intangible folk cultural property. === References === * ''Noma Jinja-shi'', Noma Jinja-shi Editorial Committee（Noma Shrine, 2007） === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Noma Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Noma Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Iyo Province]] [[Category:愛媛県の神社]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:伊予国の式内社|名のましんしや]] [[Category:愛媛県の旧県社]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Imabari, Ehime]] [[Category:History of Imabari, Ehime]] [[Category:重要文化財 (建造物)]] [[Category:愛媛県の重要文化財]] [[Category:愛媛県指定有形文化財]] [[Category:都道府県指定無形民俗文化財]][[Category:qq]]

{{wikidata link|Q11646210}} == Japanese content == {{神社 | 名称=Noma Shrine | 画像=[[File:今治野間神社2.jpg|240px|Torii]]
Torii of Noma Shrine（{{ill|lt=2025|Q49616|WD=Q49616|1=2025}}{{ill|lt=December|Q126|WD=Q126|1=December}}） | 所在地={{ill|lt=Ehime Prefecture|Q123376|WD=Q123376|1=Ehime Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Imabari|Q461020|WD=Q461020|1=Imabari, Ehime}}Kannomiya-chō 699 | 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|3|3.6|N|132|57|25.7|E|region:JP-38_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Noma Shrine}} | 祭神=Ayahayatama-no-Mikoto
Wakayao-no-Mikoto
{{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}
Noma-hime-no-Mikoto | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}（{{ill|lt=myōjin taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|lt=myōjin-tai|1=Myōjin Taisha}}）・{{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=Ken-sha}} | 創建=Unknown | 本殿={{ill|lt=irimoya-zukuri|Q855025|WD=Q855025|1=East Asian hip-and-gable roof}} | 例祭={{ill|lt=May 3|Q2547|WD=Q2547|1=May 3}} | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Ehime|label=Noma Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Noma Shrine'''（のまじんじゃ）is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Kannomiya, {{ill|lt=Imabari|Q461020|WD=Q461020|1=Imabari, Ehime}}, {{ill|lt=Ehime Prefecture|Q123376|WD=Q123376|1=Ehime Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}（{{ill|lt=myōjin taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|lt=myōjin-tai|1=Myōjin Taisha}}）, and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=Ken-sha}}. === Enshrined deities === *{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ayahayatama-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} *{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Wakayao-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} *{{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} *{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Noma-hime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} There are multiple theories about the enshrined deities, but these four are currently treated as the shrine's deities. Ayahayatama-no-Mikoto is the deity of {{ill|lt=Hayatama Shrine|Q11639721|WD=Q11639721|1=Hayatani Shrine}} and ancestor of the {{ill|lt=Aki-no-kuni-no-miyatsuko|Q21654103|WD=Q21654103|lt=provincial governors of Aki|1=provincial governors of Aki}}. Wakayao-no-Mikoto is his third-generation descendant and ancestor of the {{ill|lt=Nukata-no-kuni-no-miyatsuko|Q21654103|WD=Q21654103|1=Nukata-no-kuni-no-miyatsuko}}. Noma-hime-no-Mikoto is regarded as Wakayao-no-Mikoto's wife. Because Noma Shrine was called "Noma Tennō-gami" and "Nōman Tennō-gami," there is also a theory that its deity was {{ill|lt=Gozu Tennō|Q11570247|WD=Q11570247|1=Gozu Tennō}}, with {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} as principal deity. However, ''Noma Jinja-shi'' considers Ayahayatama-no-Mikoto, Wakayao-no-Mikoto, and Noma-hime-no-Mikoto to be the main deities, and Susanoo-no-Mikoto to have been an associated deity. That there was a medieval period when {{ill|lt=Gozu Tennō|Q11570247|WD=Q11570247|1=Gozu Tennō}} was enshrined is clear from documents remaining at the temple-bettō {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Jōzen-ji|1=UNKNOWN}}. === Origins === Its founding is unknown, but it is believed to have begun when Wakayao-no-Mikoto, who became {{ill|lt=Nukata-no-kuni-no-miyatsuko|Q21654103|WD=Q21654103|1=Nukata-no-kuni-no-miyatsuko}}, worshiped his ancestor {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ayahayatama-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}. In the ''{{ill|lt=Kuni no miyatsuko hongi|Q2716751|WD=Q2716751|lt=Sendai Kuji Hongi: Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi|1=Kujiki}}'', Wakayao-no-Mikoto is said to have been appointed provincial governor in the reign of {{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|WD=Q232803|1=Empress Jingū}}. According to shrine tradition, a shrine building was constructed in {{ill|lt=701|Q23496|WD=Q23496|1=701}}（{{ill|lt=Taihō|Q1346269|WD=Q1346269|lt=Taihō|1=Taihō (era)}} 1）. In {{ill|lt=766|Q30564|WD=Q30564|1=766}}（{{ill|lt=Tenpyō-jingo|Q1373181|WD=Q1373181|1=Tenpyō-jingo}} 2）, it was granted Junior Fifth Rank, Lower and given two households of shrine serfs. In {{ill|lt=837|Q25093|WD=Q25093|1=837}}（{{ill|lt=Jōwa|Q1075605|WD=Q1075605|lt=Jōwa|1=Jōwa (Heian period)}} 4）, together with {{ill|lt=Ōyamazumi Shrine|Q703904|WD=Q703904|1=Ōyamazumi Shrine}}, it received myōjin status for the first time in the {{ill|lt=Nankaidō|Q1376747|WD=Q1376747|1=Nankaidō}} region. In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}, it is listed as a {{ill|lt=myōjin taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}}, and in {{ill|lt=940|Q23830|WD=Q23830|1=940}}（{{ill|lt=Tengyō|Q1327340|WD=Q1327340|1=Tengyō}} 3）, due to prayers against pirate raiding, it was promoted to Senior Second Rank. In the medieval period it was strongly revered by military leaders, especially the {{ill|lt=Kōno clan|Q11554232|WD=Q11554232|1=Kōno clan}}. In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, it served as a {{ill|lt=rainmaking|Q477738|WD=Q477738|lt=rain-prayer site|1=Rainmaking (ritual)}} for the {{ill|lt=Matsuyama Domain|Q1042087|WD=Q1042087|lt=Matsuyama domain of Iyo|1=Iyo-Matsuyama Domain}}. In 1871（Meiji 4）it was ranked {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}, and in 1895（Meiji 28）it was promoted to {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=Ken-sha}}. === Shrine name === In sources such as the {{ill|lt=Shoku Nihongi|Q611399|WD=Q611399|1=Shoku Nihongi}}, it appears as "Noma-no-kami"; in the {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q1052981|lt=Sandai Jitsuroku|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}, as "Noma Tennō-gami"; and in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, as "Noma Shrine." The writing of "Noma" also included characters such as 乃万, 濃満, and 濃万, deriving from {{ill|lt=Noma District|Q266830|WD=Q266830|1=Noma District, Ehime}}（{{ill|lt=Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi|Q133833194|WD=Q133833194|1=Kokuzo Hongi}}'s {{ill|lt=Nukata Province|Q21654103|WD=Q21654103|lt=Nukata-no-kuni|1=Nukata-no-kuni}}）. In the Kenchō-era documents it appears as "Nomangu," and in Kōfuku-ji's solicited-deity register as "Shōichii Nōman Tennō-gami." There are also examples such as "Daibonten'ō-gū" and "Nomasha Daibonten'ō-gū." Among local people it was called "Tennō-sha" or "Tennō-gū," and popularly known as "Tennon-san." 今治野間神社.jpg|Sanmon（{{ill|lt=2025|Q49616|WD=Q49616|1=2025}}{{ill|lt=December|Q126|WD=Q126|1=December}}） 今治野間神社4.jpg|Hokora（December 2025） 今治野間神社5.jpg|Honden（December 2025） 今治野間神社6.jpg|Stone steps（December 2025） === Cultural properties === ;Important Cultural Property *Stone {{ill|lt=Hōkyōintō|Q3146365|WD=Q3146365|1=Hōkyōintō}} built in {{ill|lt=1322|Q5770|WD=Q5770|1=1322}}（{{ill|lt=Genkō|Q509889|WD=Q509889|1=Genkō (1321–1324)}} 2） ;Prefectural Tangible Cultural Property *11 {{ill|lt=Japanese bronze mirrors|Q1041984|WD=Q1041984|lt=wa-kyō|1=Bronze mirror}} ;Prefectural Intangible Folk Cultural Property *{{ill|lt=Shishimai|Q713866|WD=Q713866|1=Lion dance}} transmitted at Kannomiya Noma Shrine 今治野間神社3.jpg|Important Cultural Property stone {{ill|lt=Hōkyōintō|Q3146365|WD=Q3146365|1=Hōkyōintō}} behind the honden（December 2025） === Annual festival === At the annual grand festival held on May 3, dedicatory performances include the Urayasu dance, daimyo procession, lion dance, mikoshi procession, and hemp-plain sacred boat. Among these, the {{ill|lt=lion dance|Q713866|WD=Q713866|1=Lion dance}} is designated an {{ill|lt=Ehime Prefecture|Q123376|WD=Q123376|1=Ehime Prefecture}} intangible folk cultural property, and the mikoshi procession is designated an {{ill|lt=Imabari|Q461020|WD=Q461020|1=Imabari, Ehime}} intangible folk cultural property. === References === * ''Noma Jinja-shi'', Noma Jinja-shi Editorial Committee（Noma Shrine, 2007） === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Noma Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Noma Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Iyo Province]] [[Category:愛媛県の神社]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Iyo Province]] [[Category:愛媛県の旧県社]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Imabari, Ehime]] [[Category:History of Imabari, Ehime]] [[Category:重要文化財 (建造物)]] [[Category:愛媛県の重要文化財]] [[Category:愛媛県指定有形文化財]] [[Category:都道府県指定無形民俗文化財]][[Category:qq]]

Torii of Noma Shrine（{{ill|lt=2025|Q49616|WD=Q49616|1=2025}}{{ill|lt=December|Q126|WD=Q126|1=December}}） | 所在地={{ill|lt=Ehime Prefecture|Q123376|WD=Q123376|1=Ehime Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Imabari|Q461020|WD=Q461020|1=Imabari, Ehime}}Kannomiya-chō 699 | 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|3|3.6|N|132|57|25.7|E|region:JP-38_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Noma Shrine}} | 祭神=Ayahayatama-no-Mikoto
Wakayao-no-Mikoto
{{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}
Noma-hime-no-Mikoto | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}（{{ill|lt=myōjin taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|lt=myōjin-tai|1=Myōjin Taisha}}）・{{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=Ken-sha}} | 創建=Unknown | 本殿={{ill|lt=irimoya-zukuri|Q855025|WD=Q855025|1=East Asian hip-and-gable roof}} | 例祭={{ill|lt=May 3|Q2547|WD=Q2547|1=May 3}} | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Ehime|label=Noma Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Noma Shrine'''（のまじんじゃ）is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Kannomiya, {{ill|lt=Imabari|Q461020|WD=Q461020|1=Imabari, Ehime}}, {{ill|lt=Ehime Prefecture|Q123376|WD=Q123376|1=Ehime Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}（{{ill|lt=myōjin taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|lt=myōjin-tai|1=Myōjin Taisha}}）, and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=Ken-sha}}. === Enshrined deities === *{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ayahayatama-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} *{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Wakayao-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} *{{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} *{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Noma-hime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} There are multiple theories about the enshrined deities, but these four are currently treated as the shrine's deities. Ayahayatama-no-Mikoto is the deity of {{ill|lt=Hayatama Shrine|Q11639721|WD=Q11639721|1=Hayatani Shrine}} and ancestor of the {{ill|lt=Aki-no-kuni-no-miyatsuko|Q21654103|WD=Q21654103|lt=provincial governors of Aki|1=provincial governors of Aki}}. Wakayao-no-Mikoto is his third-generation descendant and ancestor of the {{ill|lt=Nukata-no-kuni-no-miyatsuko|Q21654103|WD=Q21654103|1=Nukata-no-kuni-no-miyatsuko}}. Noma-hime-no-Mikoto is regarded as Wakayao-no-Mikoto's wife. Because Noma Shrine was called "Noma Tennō-gami" and "Nōman Tennō-gami," there is also a theory that its deity was {{ill|lt=Gozu Tennō|Q11570247|WD=Q11570247|1=Gozu Tennō}}, with {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} as principal deity. However, ''Noma Jinja-shi'' considers Ayahayatama-no-Mikoto, Wakayao-no-Mikoto, and Noma-hime-no-Mikoto to be the main deities, and Susanoo-no-Mikoto to have been an associated deity. That there was a medieval period when {{ill|lt=Gozu Tennō|Q11570247|WD=Q11570247|1=Gozu Tennō}} was enshrined is clear from documents remaining at the temple-bettō {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Jōzen-ji|1=UNKNOWN}}. === Origins === Its founding is unknown, but it is believed to have begun when Wakayao-no-Mikoto, who became {{ill|lt=Nukata-no-kuni-no-miyatsuko|Q21654103|WD=Q21654103|1=Nukata-no-kuni-no-miyatsuko}}, worshiped his ancestor {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ayahayatama-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}. In the ''{{ill|lt=Kuni no miyatsuko hongi|Q2716751|WD=Q2716751|lt=Sendai Kuji Hongi: Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi|1=Kujiki}}'', Wakayao-no-Mikoto is said to have been appointed provincial governor in the reign of {{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|WD=Q232803|1=Empress Jingū}}. According to shrine tradition, a shrine building was constructed in {{ill|lt=701|Q23496|WD=Q23496|1=701}}（{{ill|lt=Taihō|Q1346269|WD=Q1346269|lt=Taihō|1=Taihō (era)}} 1）. In {{ill|lt=766|Q30564|WD=Q30564|1=766}}（{{ill|lt=Tenpyō-jingo|Q1373181|WD=Q1373181|1=Tenpyō-jingo}} 2）, it was granted Junior Fifth Rank, Lower and given two households of shrine serfs. In {{ill|lt=837|Q25093|WD=Q25093|1=837}}（{{ill|lt=Jōwa|Q1075605|WD=Q1075605|lt=Jōwa|1=Jōwa (Heian period)}} 4）, together with {{ill|lt=Ōyamazumi Shrine|Q703904|WD=Q703904|1=Ōyamazumi Shrine}}, it received myōjin status for the first time in the {{ill|lt=Nankaidō|Q1376747|WD=Q1376747|1=Nankaidō}} region. In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}, it is listed as a {{ill|lt=myōjin taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}}, and in {{ill|lt=940|Q23830|WD=Q23830|1=940}}（{{ill|lt=Tengyō|Q1327340|WD=Q1327340|1=Tengyō}} 3）, due to prayers against pirate raiding, it was promoted to Senior Second Rank. In the medieval period it was strongly revered by military leaders, especially the {{ill|lt=Kōno clan|Q11554232|WD=Q11554232|1=Kōno clan}}. In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, it served as a {{ill|lt=rainmaking|Q477738|WD=Q477738|lt=rain-prayer site|1=Rainmaking (ritual)}} for the {{ill|lt=Matsuyama Domain|Q1042087|WD=Q1042087|lt=Matsuyama domain of Iyo|1=Iyo-Matsuyama Domain}}. In 1871（Meiji 4）it was ranked {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}, and in 1895（Meiji 28）it was promoted to {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=Ken-sha}}. === Shrine name === In sources such as the {{ill|lt=Shoku Nihongi|Q611399|WD=Q611399|1=Shoku Nihongi}}, it appears as "Noma-no-kami"; in the {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q1052981|lt=Sandai Jitsuroku|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}, as "Noma Tennō-gami"; and in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, as "Noma Shrine." The writing of "Noma" also included characters such as 乃万, 濃満, and 濃万, deriving from {{ill|lt=Noma District|Q266830|WD=Q266830|1=Noma District, Ehime}}（{{ill|lt=Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi|Q133833194|WD=Q133833194|1=Kokuzo Hongi}}'s {{ill|lt=Nukata Province|Q21654103|WD=Q21654103|lt=Nukata-no-kuni|1=Nukata-no-kuni}}）. In the Kenchō-era documents it appears as "Nomangu," and in Kōfuku-ji's solicited-deity register as "Shōichii Nōman Tennō-gami." There are also examples such as "Daibonten'ō-gū" and "Nomasha Daibonten'ō-gū." Among local people it was called "Tennō-sha" or "Tennō-gū," and popularly known as "Tennon-san." 今治野間神社.jpg|Sanmon（{{ill|lt=2025|Q49616|WD=Q49616|1=2025}}{{ill|lt=December|Q126|WD=Q126|1=December}}） 今治野間神社4.jpg|Hokora（December 2025） 今治野間神社5.jpg|Honden（December 2025） 今治野間神社6.jpg|Stone steps（December 2025） === Cultural properties === ;Important Cultural Property *Stone {{ill|lt=Hōkyōintō|Q3146365|WD=Q3146365|1=Hōkyōintō}} built in {{ill|lt=1322|Q5770|WD=Q5770|1=1322}}（{{ill|lt=Genkō|Q509889|WD=Q509889|1=Genkō (1321–1324)}} 2） ;Prefectural Tangible Cultural Property *11 {{ill|lt=Japanese bronze mirrors|Q1041984|WD=Q1041984|lt=wa-kyō|1=Bronze mirror}} ;Prefectural Intangible Folk Cultural Property *{{ill|lt=Shishimai|Q713866|WD=Q713866|1=Lion dance}} transmitted at Kannomiya Noma Shrine 今治野間神社3.jpg|Important Cultural Property stone {{ill|lt=Hōkyōintō|Q3146365|WD=Q3146365|1=Hōkyōintō}} behind the honden（December 2025） === Annual festival === At the annual grand festival held on May 3, dedicatory performances include the Urayasu dance, daimyo procession, lion dance, mikoshi procession, and hemp-plain sacred boat. Among these, the {{ill|lt=lion dance|Q713866|WD=Q713866|1=Lion dance}} is designated an {{ill|lt=Ehime Prefecture|Q123376|WD=Q123376|1=Ehime Prefecture}} intangible folk cultural property, and the mikoshi procession is designated an {{ill|lt=Imabari|Q461020|WD=Q461020|1=Imabari, Ehime}} intangible folk cultural property. === References === * ''Noma Jinja-shi'', Noma Jinja-shi Editorial Committee（Noma Shrine, 2007） === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Noma Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Noma Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Iyo Province]] [[Category:愛媛県の神社]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Iyo Province]] [[Category:愛媛県の旧県社]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Imabari, Ehime]] [[Category:History of Imabari, Ehime]] [[Category:重要文化財 (建造物)]] [[Category:愛媛県の重要文化財]] [[Category:愛媛県指定有形文化財]] [[Category:都道府県指定無形民俗文化財]][[Category:qq]]

Wakayao-no-Mikoto is his third-generation descendant and ancestor of the {{ill|lt=Nukata-no-kuni-no-miyatsuko|Q21654103|WD=Q21654103|1=Nukata-no-kuni-no-miyatsuko}}. Noma-hime-no-Mikoto is regarded as Wakayao-no-Mikoto's wife. Because Noma Shrine was called "Noma Tennō-gami" and "Nōman Tennō-gami," there is also a theory that its deity was {{ill|lt=Gozu Tennō|Q11570247|WD=Q11570247|1=Gozu Tennō}}, with {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} as principal deity. However, ''Noma Jinja-shi'' considers Ayahayatama-no-Mikoto, Wakayao-no-Mikoto, and Noma-hime-no-Mikoto to be the main deities, and Susanoo-no-Mikoto to have been an associated deity. That there was a medieval period when {{ill|lt=Gozu Tennō|Q11570247|WD=Q11570247|1=Gozu Tennō}} was enshrined is clear from documents remaining at the temple-bettō {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Jōzen-ji|1=UNKNOWN}}. === Origins === Its founding is unknown, but it is believed to have begun when Wakayao-no-Mikoto, who became {{ill|lt=Nukata-no-kuni-no-miyatsuko|Q21654103|WD=Q21654103|1=Nukata-no-kuni-no-miyatsuko}}, worshiped his ancestor {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ayahayatama-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}. In the ''{{ill|lt=Kuni no miyatsuko hongi|Q2716751|WD=Q2716751|lt=Sendai Kuji Hongi: Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi|1=Kujiki}}'', Wakayao-no-Mikoto is said to have been appointed provincial governor in the reign of {{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|WD=Q232803|1=Empress Jingū}}. According to shrine tradition, a shrine building was constructed in {{ill|lt=701|Q23496|WD=Q23496|1=701}}（{{ill|lt=Taihō|Q1346269|WD=Q1346269|lt=Taihō|1=Taihō (era)}} 1）. In {{ill|lt=766|Q30564|WD=Q30564|1=766}}（{{ill|lt=Tenpyō-jingo|Q1373181|WD=Q1373181|1=Tenpyō-jingo}} 2）, it was granted Junior Fifth Rank, Lower and given two households of shrine serfs. In {{ill|lt=837|Q25093|WD=Q25093|1=837}}（{{ill|lt=Jōwa|Q1075605|WD=Q1075605|lt=Jōwa|1=Jōwa (Heian period)}} 4）, together with {{ill|lt=Ōyamazumi Shrine|Q703904|WD=Q703904|1=Ōyamazumi Shrine}}, it received myōjin status for the first time in the {{ill|lt=Nankaidō|Q1376747|WD=Q1376747|1=Nankaidō}} region. In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}, it is listed as a {{ill|lt=myōjin taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}}, and in {{ill|lt=940|Q23830|WD=Q23830|1=940}}（{{ill|lt=Tengyō|Q1327340|WD=Q1327340|1=Tengyō}} 3）, due to prayers against pirate raiding, it was promoted to Senior Second Rank. In the medieval period it was strongly revered by military leaders, especially the {{ill|lt=Kōno clan|Q11554232|WD=Q11554232|1=Kōno clan}}. In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, it served as a {{ill|lt=rainmaking|Q477738|WD=Q477738|lt=rain-prayer site|1=Rainmaking (ritual)}} for the {{ill|lt=Matsuyama Domain|Q1042087|WD=Q1042087|lt=Matsuyama domain of Iyo|1=Iyo-Matsuyama Domain}}. In 1871（Meiji 4）it was ranked {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}, and in 1895（Meiji 28）it was promoted to {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=Ken-sha}}. === Shrine name === In sources such as the {{ill|lt=Shoku Nihongi|Q611399|WD=Q611399|1=Shoku Nihongi}}, it appears as "Noma-no-kami"; in the {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q1052981|lt=Sandai Jitsuroku|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}, as "Noma Tennō-gami"; and in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, as "Noma Shrine." The writing of "Noma" also included characters such as 乃万, 濃満, and 濃万, deriving from {{ill|lt=Noma District|Q266830|WD=Q266830|1=Noma District, Ehime}}（{{ill|lt=Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi|Q133833194|WD=Q133833194|1=Kokuzo Hongi}}'s {{ill|lt=Nukata Province|Q21654103|WD=Q21654103|lt=Nukata-no-kuni|1=Nukata-no-kuni}}）. In the Kenchō-era documents it appears as "Nomangu," and in Kōfuku-ji's solicited-deity register as "Shōichii Nōman Tennō-gami." There are also examples such as "Daibonten'ō-gū" and "Nomasha Daibonten'ō-gū." Among local people it was called "Tennō-sha" or "Tennō-gū," and popularly known as "Tennon-san." 今治野間神社.jpg|Sanmon（{{ill|lt=2025|Q49616|WD=Q49616|1=2025}}{{ill|lt=December|Q126|WD=Q126|1=December}}） 今治野間神社4.jpg|Hokora（December 2025） 今治野間神社5.jpg|Honden（December 2025） 今治野間神社6.jpg|Stone steps（December 2025） === Cultural properties === ;Important Cultural Property *Stone {{ill|lt=Hōkyōintō|Q3146365|WD=Q3146365|1=Hōkyōintō}} built in {{ill|lt=1322|Q5770|WD=Q5770|1=1322}}（{{ill|lt=Genkō|Q509889|WD=Q509889|1=Genkō (1321–1324)}} 2） ;Prefectural Tangible Cultural Property *11 {{ill|lt=Japanese bronze mirrors|Q1041984|WD=Q1041984|lt=wa-kyō|1=Bronze mirror}} ;Prefectural Intangible Folk Cultural Property *{{ill|lt=Shishimai|Q713866|WD=Q713866|1=Lion dance}} transmitted at Kannomiya Noma Shrine 今治野間神社3.jpg|Important Cultural Property stone {{ill|lt=Hōkyōintō|Q3146365|WD=Q3146365|1=Hōkyōintō}} behind the honden（December 2025） === Annual festival === At the annual grand festival held on May 3, dedicatory performances include the Urayasu dance, daimyo procession, lion dance, mikoshi procession, and hemp-plain sacred boat. Among these, the {{ill|lt=lion dance|Q713866|WD=Q713866|1=Lion dance}} is designated an {{ill|lt=Ehime Prefecture|Q123376|WD=Q123376|1=Ehime Prefecture}} intangible folk cultural property, and the mikoshi procession is designated an {{ill|lt=Imabari|Q461020|WD=Q461020|1=Imabari, Ehime}} intangible folk cultural property. === References === * ''Noma Jinja-shi'', Noma Jinja-shi Editorial Committee（Noma Shrine, 2007） === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Noma Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Noma Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Iyo Province]] [[Category:愛媛県の神社]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Iyo Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Ehime Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Imabari, Ehime]] [[Category:History of Imabari, Ehime]] [[Category:重要文化財 (建造物)]] [[Category:愛媛県の重要文化財]] [[Category:愛媛県指定有形文化財]] [[Category:都道府県指定無形民俗文化財]][[Category:qq]]

It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}（{{ill|lt=myōjin taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|lt=myōjin-tai|1=Myōjin Taisha}}）, and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=Ken-sha}}. === Enshrined deities === *{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ayahayatama-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} *{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Wakayao-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} *{{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} *{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Noma-hime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} There are multiple theories about the enshrined deities, but these four are currently treated as the shrine's deities. Ayahayatama-no-Mikoto is the deity of {{ill|lt=Hayatama Shrine|Q11639721|WD=Q11639721|1=Hayatani Shrine}} and ancestor of the {{ill|lt=Aki-no-kuni-no-miyatsuko|Q21654103|WD=Q21654103|lt=provincial governors of Aki|1=provincial governors of Aki}}. Wakayao-no-Mikoto is his third-generation descendant and ancestor of the {{ill|lt=Nukata-no-kuni-no-miyatsuko|Q21654103|WD=Q21654103|1=Nukata-no-kuni-no-miyatsuko}}. Noma-hime-no-Mikoto is regarded as Wakayao-no-Mikoto's wife. Because Noma Shrine was called "Noma Tennō-gami" and "Nōman Tennō-gami," there is also a theory that its deity was {{ill|lt=Gozu Tennō|Q11570247|WD=Q11570247|1=Gozu Tennō}}, with {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} as principal deity. However, ''Noma Jinja-shi'' considers Ayahayatama-no-Mikoto, Wakayao-no-Mikoto, and Noma-hime-no-Mikoto to be the main deities, and Susanoo-no-Mikoto to have been an associated deity. That there was a medieval period when {{ill|lt=Gozu Tennō|Q11570247|WD=Q11570247|1=Gozu Tennō}} was enshrined is clear from documents remaining at the temple-bettō {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Jōzen-ji|1=UNKNOWN}}. === Origins === Its founding is unknown, but it is believed to have begun when Wakayao-no-Mikoto, who became {{ill|lt=Nukata-no-kuni-no-miyatsuko|Q21654103|WD=Q21654103|1=Nukata-no-kuni-no-miyatsuko}}, worshiped his ancestor {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ayahayatama-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}. In the ''{{ill|lt=Kuni no miyatsuko hongi|Q2716751|WD=Q2716751|lt=Sendai Kuji Hongi: Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi|1=Kujiki}}'', Wakayao-no-Mikoto is said to have been appointed provincial governor in the reign of {{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|WD=Q232803|1=Empress Jingū}}. According to shrine tradition, a shrine building was constructed in {{ill|lt=701|Q23496|WD=Q23496|1=701}}（{{ill|lt=Taihō|Q1346269|WD=Q1346269|lt=Taihō|1=Taihō (era)}} 1）. In {{ill|lt=766|Q30564|WD=Q30564|1=766}}（{{ill|lt=Tenpyō-jingo|Q1373181|WD=Q1373181|1=Tenpyō-jingo}} 2）, it was granted Junior Fifth Rank, Lower and given two households of shrine serfs. In {{ill|lt=837|Q25093|WD=Q25093|1=837}}（{{ill|lt=Jōwa|Q1075605|WD=Q1075605|lt=Jōwa|1=Jōwa (Heian period)}} 4）, together with {{ill|lt=Ōyamazumi Shrine|Q703904|WD=Q703904|1=Ōyamazumi Shrine}}, it received myōjin status for the first time in the {{ill|lt=Nankaidō|Q1376747|WD=Q1376747|1=Nankaidō}} region. In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}, it is listed as a {{ill|lt=myōjin taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}}, and in {{ill|lt=940|Q23830|WD=Q23830|1=940}}（{{ill|lt=Tengyō|Q1327340|WD=Q1327340|1=Tengyō}} 3）, due to prayers against pirate raiding, it was promoted to Senior Second Rank. In the medieval period it was strongly revered by military leaders, especially the {{ill|lt=Kōno clan|Q11554232|WD=Q11554232|1=Kōno clan}}. In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, it served as a {{ill|lt=rainmaking|Q477738|WD=Q477738|lt=rain-prayer site|1=Rainmaking (ritual)}} for the {{ill|lt=Matsuyama Domain|Q1042087|WD=Q1042087|lt=Matsuyama domain of Iyo|1=Iyo-Matsuyama Domain}}. In 1871（Meiji 4）it was ranked {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}, and in 1895（Meiji 28）it was promoted to {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=Ken-sha}}. === Shrine name === In sources such as the {{ill|lt=Shoku Nihongi|Q611399|WD=Q611399|1=Shoku Nihongi}}, it appears as "Noma-no-kami"; in the {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q1052981|lt=Sandai Jitsuroku|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}, as "Noma Tennō-gami"; and in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, as "Noma Shrine." The writing of "Noma" also included characters such as 乃万, 濃満, and 濃万, deriving from {{ill|lt=Noma District|Q266830|WD=Q266830|1=Noma District, Ehime}}（{{ill|lt=Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi|Q133833194|WD=Q133833194|1=Kokuzo Hongi}}'s {{ill|lt=Nukata Province|Q21654103|WD=Q21654103|lt=Nukata-no-kuni|1=Nukata-no-kuni}}）. In the Kenchō-era documents it appears as "Nomangu," and in Kōfuku-ji's solicited-deity register as "Shōichii Nōman Tennō-gami." There are also examples such as "Daibonten'ō-gū" and "Nomasha Daibonten'ō-gū." Among local people it was called "Tennō-sha" or "Tennō-gū," and popularly known as "Tennon-san." 今治野間神社.jpg|Sanmon（{{ill|lt=2025|Q49616|WD=Q49616|1=2025}}{{ill|lt=December|Q126|WD=Q126|1=December}}） 今治野間神社4.jpg|Hokora（December 2025） 今治野間神社5.jpg|Honden（December 2025） 今治野間神社6.jpg|Stone steps（December 2025） === Cultural properties === ;Important Cultural Property *Stone {{ill|lt=Hōkyōintō|Q3146365|WD=Q3146365|1=Hōkyōintō}} built in {{ill|lt=1322|Q5770|WD=Q5770|1=1322}}（{{ill|lt=Genkō|Q509889|WD=Q509889|1=Genkō (1321–1324)}} 2） ;Prefectural Tangible Cultural Property *11 {{ill|lt=Japanese bronze mirrors|Q1041984|WD=Q1041984|lt=wa-kyō|1=Bronze mirror}} ;Prefectural Intangible Folk Cultural Property *{{ill|lt=Shishimai|Q713866|WD=Q713866|1=Lion dance}} transmitted at Kannomiya Noma Shrine 今治野間神社3.jpg|Important Cultural Property stone {{ill|lt=Hōkyōintō|Q3146365|WD=Q3146365|1=Hōkyōintō}} behind the honden（December 2025） === Annual festival === At the annual grand festival held on May 3, dedicatory performances include the Urayasu dance, daimyo procession, lion dance, mikoshi procession, and hemp-plain sacred boat. Among these, the {{ill|lt=lion dance|Q713866|WD=Q713866|1=Lion dance}} is designated an {{ill|lt=Ehime Prefecture|Q123376|WD=Q123376|1=Ehime Prefecture}} intangible folk cultural property, and the mikoshi procession is designated an {{ill|lt=Imabari|Q461020|WD=Q461020|1=Imabari, Ehime}} intangible folk cultural property. === References === * ''Noma Jinja-shi'', Noma Jinja-shi Editorial Committee（Noma Shrine, 2007） === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Noma Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Noma Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Iyo Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ehime Prefecture]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Iyo Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Ehime Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Imabari, Ehime]] [[Category:History of Imabari, Ehime]] [[Category:重要文化財 (建造物)]] [[Category:愛媛県の重要文化財]] [[Category:愛媛県指定有形文化財]] [[Category:都道府県指定無形民俗文化財]][[Category:qq]]

Torii of Noma Shrine（{{ill|lt=2025|Q49616|WD=Q49616|1=2025}}{{ill|lt=December|Q126|WD=Q126|1=December}}） | 所在地={{ill|lt=Ehime Prefecture|Q123376|WD=Q123376|1=Ehime Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Imabari|Q461020|WD=Q461020|1=Imabari, Ehime}}Kannomiya-chō 699 | 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|3|3.6|N|132|57|25.7|E|region:JP-38_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Noma Shrine}} | 祭神=Ayahayatama-no-Mikoto
Wakayao-no-Mikoto
{{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}
Noma-hime-no-Mikoto | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}（{{ill|lt=myōjin taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|lt=myōjin-tai|1=Myōjin Taisha}}）・{{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=Ken-sha}} | 創建=Unknown | 本殿={{ill|lt=irimoya-zukuri|Q855025|WD=Q855025|1=East Asian hip-and-gable roof}} | 例祭={{ill|lt=May 3|Q2547|WD=Q2547|1=May 3}} | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Ehime|label=Noma Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Noma Shrine'''（のまじんじゃ）is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Kannomiya, {{ill|lt=Imabari|Q461020|WD=Q461020|1=Imabari, Ehime}}, {{ill|lt=Ehime Prefecture|Q123376|WD=Q123376|1=Ehime Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}（{{ill|lt=myōjin taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|lt=myōjin-tai|1=Myōjin Taisha}}）, and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=Ken-sha}}. === Enshrined deities === *{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ayahayatama-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} *{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Wakayao-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} *{{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} *{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Noma-hime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} There are multiple theories about the enshrined deities, but these four are currently treated as the shrine's deities. Ayahayatama-no-Mikoto is the deity of {{ill|lt=Hayatama Shrine|Q11639721|WD=Q11639721|1=Hayatani Shrine}} and ancestor of the {{ill|lt=Aki-no-kuni-no-miyatsuko|Q21654103|WD=Q21654103|lt=provincial governors of Aki|1=provincial governors of Aki}}. Wakayao-no-Mikoto is his third-generation descendant and ancestor of the {{ill|lt=Nukata-no-kuni-no-miyatsuko|Q21654103|WD=Q21654103|1=Nukata-no-kuni-no-miyatsuko}}. Noma-hime-no-Mikoto is regarded as Wakayao-no-Mikoto's wife. Because Noma Shrine was called "Noma Tennō-gami" and "Nōman Tennō-gami," there is also a theory that its deity was {{ill|lt=Gozu Tennō|Q11570247|WD=Q11570247|1=Gozu Tennō}}, with {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} as principal deity. However, ''Noma Jinja-shi'' considers Ayahayatama-no-Mikoto, Wakayao-no-Mikoto, and Noma-hime-no-Mikoto to be the main deities, and Susanoo-no-Mikoto to have been an associated deity. That there was a medieval period when {{ill|lt=Gozu Tennō|Q11570247|WD=Q11570247|1=Gozu Tennō}} was enshrined is clear from documents remaining at the temple-bettō {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Jōzen-ji|1=UNKNOWN}}. === Origins === Its founding is unknown, but it is believed to have begun when Wakayao-no-Mikoto, who became {{ill|lt=Nukata-no-kuni-no-miyatsuko|Q21654103|WD=Q21654103|1=Nukata-no-kuni-no-miyatsuko}}, worshiped his ancestor {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ayahayatama-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}. In the ''{{ill|lt=Kuni no miyatsuko hongi|Q2716751|WD=Q2716751|lt=Sendai Kuji Hongi: Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi|1=Kujiki}}'', Wakayao-no-Mikoto is said to have been appointed provincial governor in the reign of {{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|WD=Q232803|1=Empress Jingū}}. According to shrine tradition, a shrine building was constructed in {{ill|lt=701|Q23496|WD=Q23496|1=701}}（{{ill|lt=Taihō|Q1346269|WD=Q1346269|lt=Taihō|1=Taihō (era)}} 1）. In {{ill|lt=766|Q30564|WD=Q30564|1=766}}（{{ill|lt=Tenpyō-jingo|Q1373181|WD=Q1373181|1=Tenpyō-jingo}} 2）, it was granted Junior Fifth Rank, Lower and given two households of shrine serfs. In {{ill|lt=837|Q25093|WD=Q25093|1=837}}（{{ill|lt=Jōwa|Q1075605|WD=Q1075605|lt=Jōwa|1=Jōwa (Heian period)}} 4）, together with {{ill|lt=Ōyamazumi Shrine|Q703904|WD=Q703904|1=Ōyamazumi Shrine}}, it received myōjin status for the first time in the {{ill|lt=Nankaidō|Q1376747|WD=Q1376747|1=Nankaidō}} region. In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}, it is listed as a {{ill|lt=myōjin taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}}, and in {{ill|lt=940|Q23830|WD=Q23830|1=940}}（{{ill|lt=Tengyō|Q1327340|WD=Q1327340|1=Tengyō}} 3）, due to prayers against pirate raiding, it was promoted to Senior Second Rank. In the medieval period it was strongly revered by military leaders, especially the {{ill|lt=Kōno clan|Q11554232|WD=Q11554232|1=Kōno clan}}. In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, it served as a {{ill|lt=rainmaking|Q477738|WD=Q477738|lt=rain-prayer site|1=Rainmaking (ritual)}} for the {{ill|lt=Matsuyama Domain|Q1042087|WD=Q1042087|lt=Matsuyama domain of Iyo|1=Iyo-Matsuyama Domain}}. In 1871（Meiji 4）it was ranked {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}, and in 1895（Meiji 28）it was promoted to {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=Ken-sha}}. === Shrine name === In sources such as the {{ill|lt=Shoku Nihongi|Q611399|WD=Q611399|1=Shoku Nihongi}}, it appears as "Noma-no-kami"; in the {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q1052981|lt=Sandai Jitsuroku|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}, as "Noma Tennō-gami"; and in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, as "Noma Shrine." The writing of "Noma" also included characters such as 乃万, 濃満, and 濃万, deriving from {{ill|lt=Noma District|Q266830|WD=Q266830|1=Noma District, Ehime}}（{{ill|lt=Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi|Q133833194|WD=Q133833194|1=Kokuzo Hongi}}'s {{ill|lt=Nukata Province|Q21654103|WD=Q21654103|lt=Nukata-no-kuni|1=Nukata-no-kuni}}）. In the Kenchō-era documents it appears as "Nomangu," and in Kōfuku-ji's solicited-deity register as "Shōichii Nōman Tennō-gami." There are also examples such as "Daibonten'ō-gū" and "Nomasha Daibonten'ō-gū." Among local people it was called "Tennō-sha" or "Tennō-gū," and popularly known as "Tennon-san." 今治野間神社.jpg|Sanmon（{{ill|lt=2025|Q49616|WD=Q49616|1=2025}}{{ill|lt=December|Q126|WD=Q126|1=December}}） 今治野間神社4.jpg|Hokora（December 2025） 今治野間神社5.jpg|Honden（December 2025） 今治野間神社6.jpg|Stone steps（December 2025） === Cultural properties === ;Important Cultural Property *Stone {{ill|lt=Hōkyōintō|Q3146365|WD=Q3146365|1=Hōkyōintō}} built in {{ill|lt=1322|Q5770|WD=Q5770|1=1322}}（{{ill|lt=Genkō|Q509889|WD=Q509889|1=Genkō (1321–1324)}} 2） ;Prefectural Tangible Cultural Property *11 {{ill|lt=Japanese bronze mirrors|Q1041984|WD=Q1041984|lt=wa-kyō|1=Bronze mirror}} ;Prefectural Intangible Folk Cultural Property *{{ill|lt=Shishimai|Q713866|WD=Q713866|1=Lion dance}} transmitted at Kannomiya Noma Shrine 今治野間神社3.jpg|Important Cultural Property stone {{ill|lt=Hōkyōintō|Q3146365|WD=Q3146365|1=Hōkyōintō}} behind the honden（December 2025） === Annual festival === At the annual grand festival held on May 3, dedicatory performances include the Urayasu dance, daimyo procession, lion dance, mikoshi procession, and hemp-plain sacred boat. Among these, the {{ill|lt=lion dance|Q713866|WD=Q713866|1=Lion dance}} is designated an {{ill|lt=Ehime Prefecture|Q123376|WD=Q123376|1=Ehime Prefecture}} intangible folk cultural property, and the mikoshi procession is designated an {{ill|lt=Imabari|Q461020|WD=Q461020|1=Imabari, Ehime}} intangible folk cultural property. === References === * ''Noma Jinja-shi'', Noma Jinja-shi Editorial Committee（Noma Shrine, 2007） === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Noma Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Noma Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Iyo Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ehime Prefecture]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Iyo Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Ehime Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Imabari, Ehime]] [[Category:History of Imabari, Ehime]] [[Category:重要文化財 (建造物)]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Ehime prefecture]] [[Category:愛媛県指定有形文化財]] [[Category:都道府県指定無形民俗文化財]][[Category:qq]]

Noma-hime-no-Mikoto | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}（{{ill|lt=myōjin taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|lt=myōjin-tai|1=Myōjin Taisha}}）・{{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=Ken-sha}} | 創建=Unknown | 本殿={{ill|lt=irimoya-zukuri|Q855025|WD=Q855025|1=East Asian hip-and-gable roof}} | 例祭={{ill|lt=May 3|Q2547|WD=Q2547|1=May 3}} | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Ehime|label=Noma Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Noma Shrine'''（のまじんじゃ）is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Kannomiya, {{ill|lt=Imabari|Q461020|WD=Q461020|1=Imabari, Ehime}}, {{ill|lt=Ehime Prefecture|Q123376|WD=Q123376|1=Ehime Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}（{{ill|lt=myōjin taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|lt=myōjin-tai|1=Myōjin Taisha}}）, and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=Ken-sha}}. === Enshrined deities === *{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ayahayatama-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} *{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Wakayao-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} *{{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} *{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Noma-hime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} There are multiple theories about the enshrined deities, but these four are currently treated as the shrine's deities. Ayahayatama-no-Mikoto is the deity of {{ill|lt=Hayatama Shrine|Q11639721|WD=Q11639721|1=Hayatani Shrine}} and ancestor of the {{ill|lt=Aki-no-kuni-no-miyatsuko|Q21654103|WD=Q21654103|lt=provincial governors of Aki|1=provincial governors of Aki}}. Wakayao-no-Mikoto is his third-generation descendant and ancestor of the {{ill|lt=Nukata-no-kuni-no-miyatsuko|Q21654103|WD=Q21654103|1=Nukata-no-kuni-no-miyatsuko}}. Noma-hime-no-Mikoto is regarded as Wakayao-no-Mikoto's wife. Because Noma Shrine was called "Noma Tennō-gami" and "Nōman Tennō-gami," there is also a theory that its deity was {{ill|lt=Gozu Tennō|Q11570247|WD=Q11570247|1=Gozu Tennō}}, with {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} as principal deity. However, ''Noma Jinja-shi'' considers Ayahayatama-no-Mikoto, Wakayao-no-Mikoto, and Noma-hime-no-Mikoto to be the main deities, and Susanoo-no-Mikoto to have been an associated deity. That there was a medieval period when {{ill|lt=Gozu Tennō|Q11570247|WD=Q11570247|1=Gozu Tennō}} was enshrined is clear from documents remaining at the temple-bettō {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Jōzen-ji|1=UNKNOWN}}. === Origins === Its founding is unknown, but it is believed to have begun when Wakayao-no-Mikoto, who became {{ill|lt=Nukata-no-kuni-no-miyatsuko|Q21654103|WD=Q21654103|1=Nukata-no-kuni-no-miyatsuko}}, worshiped his ancestor {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ayahayatama-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}. In the ''{{ill|lt=Kuni no miyatsuko hongi|Q2716751|WD=Q2716751|lt=Sendai Kuji Hongi: Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi|1=Kujiki}}'', Wakayao-no-Mikoto is said to have been appointed provincial governor in the reign of {{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|WD=Q232803|1=Empress Jingū}}. According to shrine tradition, a shrine building was constructed in {{ill|lt=701|Q23496|WD=Q23496|1=701}}（{{ill|lt=Taihō|Q1346269|WD=Q1346269|lt=Taihō|1=Taihō (era)}} 1）. In {{ill|lt=766|Q30564|WD=Q30564|1=766}}（{{ill|lt=Tenpyō-jingo|Q1373181|WD=Q1373181|1=Tenpyō-jingo}} 2）, it was granted Junior Fifth Rank, Lower and given two households of shrine serfs. In {{ill|lt=837|Q25093|WD=Q25093|1=837}}（{{ill|lt=Jōwa|Q1075605|WD=Q1075605|lt=Jōwa|1=Jōwa (Heian period)}} 4）, together with {{ill|lt=Ōyamazumi Shrine|Q703904|WD=Q703904|1=Ōyamazumi Shrine}}, it received myōjin status for the first time in the {{ill|lt=Nankaidō|Q1376747|WD=Q1376747|1=Nankaidō}} region. In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}, it is listed as a {{ill|lt=myōjin taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}}, and in {{ill|lt=940|Q23830|WD=Q23830|1=940}}（{{ill|lt=Tengyō|Q1327340|WD=Q1327340|1=Tengyō}} 3）, due to prayers against pirate raiding, it was promoted to Senior Second Rank. In the medieval period it was strongly revered by military leaders, especially the {{ill|lt=Kōno clan|Q11554232|WD=Q11554232|1=Kōno clan}}. In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, it served as a {{ill|lt=rainmaking|Q477738|WD=Q477738|lt=rain-prayer site|1=Rainmaking (ritual)}} for the {{ill|lt=Matsuyama Domain|Q1042087|WD=Q1042087|lt=Matsuyama domain of Iyo|1=Iyo-Matsuyama Domain}}. In 1871（Meiji 4）it was ranked {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}, and in 1895（Meiji 28）it was promoted to {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=Ken-sha}}. === Shrine name === In sources such as the {{ill|lt=Shoku Nihongi|Q611399|WD=Q611399|1=Shoku Nihongi}}, it appears as "Noma-no-kami"; in the {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q1052981|lt=Sandai Jitsuroku|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}, as "Noma Tennō-gami"; and in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, as "Noma Shrine." The writing of "Noma" also included characters such as 乃万, 濃満, and 濃万, deriving from {{ill|lt=Noma District|Q266830|WD=Q266830|1=Noma District, Ehime}}（{{ill|lt=Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi|Q133833194|WD=Q133833194|1=Kokuzo Hongi}}'s {{ill|lt=Nukata Province|Q21654103|WD=Q21654103|lt=Nukata-no-kuni|1=Nukata-no-kuni}}）. In the Kenchō-era documents it appears as "Nomangu," and in Kōfuku-ji's solicited-deity register as "Shōichii Nōman Tennō-gami." There are also examples such as "Daibonten'ō-gū" and "Nomasha Daibonten'ō-gū." Among local people it was called "Tennō-sha" or "Tennō-gū," and popularly known as "Tennon-san." 今治野間神社.jpg|Sanmon（{{ill|lt=2025|Q49616|WD=Q49616|1=2025}}{{ill|lt=December|Q126|WD=Q126|1=December}}） 今治野間神社4.jpg|Hokora（December 2025） 今治野間神社5.jpg|Honden（December 2025） 今治野間神社6.jpg|Stone steps（December 2025） === Cultural properties === ;Important Cultural Property *Stone {{ill|lt=Hōkyōintō|Q3146365|WD=Q3146365|1=Hōkyōintō}} built in {{ill|lt=1322|Q5770|WD=Q5770|1=1322}}（{{ill|lt=Genkō|Q509889|WD=Q509889|1=Genkō (1321–1324)}} 2） ;Prefectural Tangible Cultural Property *11 {{ill|lt=Japanese bronze mirrors|Q1041984|WD=Q1041984|lt=wa-kyō|1=Bronze mirror}} ;Prefectural Intangible Folk Cultural Property *{{ill|lt=Shishimai|Q713866|WD=Q713866|1=Lion dance}} transmitted at Kannomiya Noma Shrine 今治野間神社3.jpg|Important Cultural Property stone {{ill|lt=Hōkyōintō|Q3146365|WD=Q3146365|1=Hōkyōintō}} behind the honden（December 2025） === Annual festival === At the annual grand festival held on May 3, dedicatory performances include the Urayasu dance, daimyo procession, lion dance, mikoshi procession, and hemp-plain sacred boat. Among these, the {{ill|lt=lion dance|Q713866|WD=Q713866|1=Lion dance}} is designated an {{ill|lt=Ehime Prefecture|Q123376|WD=Q123376|1=Ehime Prefecture}} intangible folk cultural property, and the mikoshi procession is designated an {{ill|lt=Imabari|Q461020|WD=Q461020|1=Imabari, Ehime}} intangible folk cultural property. === References === * ''Noma Jinja-shi'', Noma Jinja-shi Editorial Committee（Noma Shrine, 2007） === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Noma Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Noma Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Iyo Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ehime Prefecture]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Iyo Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Ehime Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Imabari, Ehime]] [[Category:History of Imabari, Ehime]] [[Category:重要文化財 (建造物)]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Ehime prefecture]] [[Category:Ehime Prefecture designated tangible cultural property]] [[Category:都道府県指定無形民俗文化財]][[Category:qq]]

In 1871（Meiji 4）it was ranked {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}, and in 1895（Meiji 28）it was promoted to {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=Ken-sha}}. === Shrine name === In sources such as the {{ill|lt=Shoku Nihongi|Q611399|WD=Q611399|1=Shoku Nihongi}}, it appears as "Noma-no-kami"; in the {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q1052981|lt=Sandai Jitsuroku|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}, as "Noma Tennō-gami"; and in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, as "Noma Shrine." In the Kenchō-era documents it appears as "Nomangu," and in Kōfuku-ji's solicited-deity register as "Shōichii Nōman Tennō-gami." There are also examples such as "Daibonten'ō-gū" and "Nomasha Daibonten'ō-gū." Among local people it was called "Tennō-sha" or "Tennō-gū," and popularly known as "Tennon-san." 今治野間神社.jpg|Sanmon（{{ill|lt=2025|Q49616|WD=Q49616|1=2025}}{{ill|lt=December|Q126|WD=Q126|1=December}}） 今治野間神社4.jpg|Hokora（December 2025） 今治野間神社5.jpg|Honden（December 2025） 今治野間神社6.jpg|Stone steps（December 2025） === Cultural properties === ;Important Cultural Property *Stone {{ill|lt=Hōkyōintō|Q3146365|WD=Q3146365|1=Hōkyōintō}} built in {{ill|lt=1322|Q5770|WD=Q5770|1=1322}}（{{ill|lt=Genkō|Q509889|WD=Q509889|1=Genkō (1321–1324)}} 2） ;Prefectural Tangible Cultural Property *11 {{ill|lt=Japanese bronze mirrors|Q1041984|WD=Q1041984|lt=wa-kyō|1=Bronze mirror}} ;Prefectural Intangible Folk Cultural Property *{{ill|lt=Shishimai|Q713866|WD=Q713866|1=Lion dance}} transmitted at Kannomiya Noma Shrine 今治野間神社3.jpg|Important Cultural Property stone {{ill|lt=Hōkyōintō|Q3146365|WD=Q3146365|1=Hōkyōintō}} behind the honden（December 2025） === Annual festival === At the annual grand festival held on May 3, dedicatory performances include the Urayasu dance, daimyo procession, lion dance, mikoshi procession, and hemp-plain sacred boat. Among these, the {{ill|lt=lion dance|Q713866|WD=Q713866|1=Lion dance}} is designated an {{ill|lt=Ehime Prefecture|Q123376|WD=Q123376|1=Ehime Prefecture}} intangible folk cultural property, and the mikoshi procession is designated an {{ill|lt=Imabari|Q461020|WD=Q461020|1=Imabari, Ehime}} intangible folk cultural property. === References === * ''Noma Jinja-shi'', Noma Jinja-shi Editorial Committee（Noma Shrine, 2007） === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Noma Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Noma Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Iyo Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ehime Prefecture]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Iyo Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Ehime Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Imabari, Ehime]] [[Category:History of Imabari, Ehime]] [[Category:重要文化財 (建造物)]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Ehime prefecture]] [[Category:Ehime Prefecture designated tangible cultural property]] [[Category:Prefecturally designated intangible folk cultural property]][[Category:qq]]

{{wikidata link|Q11646210}} == Japanese content == {{神社 | 名称=Noma Shrine | 画像=[[File:今治野間神社2.jpg|240px|Torii]]
Torii of Noma Shrine（{{ill|lt=2025|Q49616|WD=Q49616|1=2025}}{{ill|lt=December|Q126|WD=Q126|1=December}}） | 所在地={{ill|lt=Ehime Prefecture|Q123376|WD=Q123376|1=Ehime Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Imabari|Q461020|WD=Q461020|1=Imabari, Ehime}}Kannomiya-chō 699 | 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|3|3.6|N|132|57|25.7|E|region:JP-38_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Noma Shrine}} | 祭神=Ayahayatama-no-Mikoto
Wakayao-no-Mikoto
{{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}
Noma-hime-no-Mikoto | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}（{{ill|lt=myōjin taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|lt=myōjin-tai|1=Myōjin Taisha}}）・{{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=Ken-sha}} | 創建=Unknown | 本殿={{ill|lt=irimoya-zukuri|Q855025|WD=Q855025|1=East Asian hip-and-gable roof}} | 例祭={{ill|lt=May 3|Q2547|WD=Q2547|1=May 3}} | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Ehime|label=Noma Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Noma Shrine'''（のまじんじゃ）is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Kannomiya, {{ill|lt=Imabari|Q461020|WD=Q461020|1=Imabari, Ehime}}, {{ill|lt=Ehime Prefecture|Q123376|WD=Q123376|1=Ehime Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}（{{ill|lt=myōjin taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|lt=myōjin-tai|1=Myōjin Taisha}}）, and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=Ken-sha}}. === Enshrined deities === *{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ayahayatama-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} *{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Wakayao-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} *{{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} *{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Noma-hime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} There are multiple theories about the enshrined deities, but these four are currently treated as the shrine's deities. Ayahayatama-no-Mikoto is the deity of {{ill|lt=Hayatama Shrine|Q11639721|WD=Q11639721|1=Hayatani Shrine}} and ancestor of the {{ill|lt=Aki-no-kuni-no-miyatsuko|Q21654103|WD=Q21654103|lt=provincial governors of Aki|1=provincial governors of Aki}}. Wakayao-no-Mikoto is his third-generation descendant and ancestor of the {{ill|lt=Nukata-no-kuni-no-miyatsuko|Q21654103|WD=Q21654103|1=Nukata-no-kuni-no-miyatsuko}}. Noma-hime-no-Mikoto is regarded as Wakayao-no-Mikoto's wife. Because Noma Shrine was called "Noma Tennō-gami" and "Nōman Tennō-gami," there is also a theory that its deity was {{ill|lt=Gozu Tennō|Q11570247|WD=Q11570247|1=Gozu Tennō}}, with {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} as principal deity. However, ''Noma Jinja-shi'' considers Ayahayatama-no-Mikoto, Wakayao-no-Mikoto, and Noma-hime-no-Mikoto to be the main deities, and Susanoo-no-Mikoto to have been an associated deity. That there was a medieval period when {{ill|lt=Gozu Tennō|Q11570247|WD=Q11570247|1=Gozu Tennō}} was enshrined is clear from documents remaining at the temple-bettō {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Jōzen-ji|1=UNKNOWN}}. === Origins === Its founding is unknown, but it is believed to have begun when Wakayao-no-Mikoto, who became {{ill|lt=Nukata-no-kuni-no-miyatsuko|Q21654103|WD=Q21654103|1=Nukata-no-kuni-no-miyatsuko}}, worshiped his ancestor {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ayahayatama-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}. In the ''{{ill|lt=Kuni no miyatsuko hongi|Q2716751|WD=Q2716751|lt=Sendai Kuji Hongi: Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi|1=Kujiki}}'', Wakayao-no-Mikoto is said to have been appointed provincial governor in the reign of {{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|WD=Q232803|1=Empress Jingū}}. According to shrine tradition, a shrine building was constructed in {{ill|lt=701|Q23496|WD=Q23496|1=701}}（{{ill|lt=Taihō|Q1346269|WD=Q1346269|lt=Taihō|1=Taihō (era)}} 1）. In {{ill|lt=766|Q30564|WD=Q30564|1=766}}（{{ill|lt=Tenpyō-jingo|Q1373181|WD=Q1373181|1=Tenpyō-jingo}} 2）, it was granted Junior Fifth Rank, Lower and given two households of shrine serfs. In {{ill|lt=837|Q25093|WD=Q25093|1=837}}（{{ill|lt=Jōwa|Q1075605|WD=Q1075605|lt=Jōwa|1=Jōwa (Heian period)}} 4）, together with {{ill|lt=Ōyamazumi Shrine|Q703904|WD=Q703904|1=Ōyamazumi Shrine}}, it received myōjin status for the first time in the {{ill|lt=Nankaidō|Q1376747|WD=Q1376747|1=Nankaidō}} region. In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}, it is listed as a {{ill|lt=myōjin taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}}, and in {{ill|lt=940|Q23830|WD=Q23830|1=940}}（{{ill|lt=Tengyō|Q1327340|WD=Q1327340|1=Tengyō}} 3）, due to prayers against pirate raiding, it was promoted to Senior Second Rank. In the medieval period it was strongly revered by military leaders, especially the {{ill|lt=Kōno clan|Q11554232|WD=Q11554232|1=Kōno clan}}. In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, it served as a {{ill|lt=rainmaking|Q477738|WD=Q477738|lt=rain-prayer site|1=Rainmaking (ritual)}} for the {{ill|lt=Matsuyama Domain|Q1042087|WD=Q1042087|lt=Matsuyama domain of Iyo|1=Iyo-Matsuyama Domain}}. In 1871（Meiji 4）it was ranked {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}, and in 1895（Meiji 28）it was promoted to {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=Ken-sha}}. === Shrine name === In sources such as the {{ill|lt=Shoku Nihongi|Q611399|WD=Q611399|1=Shoku Nihongi}}, it appears as "Noma-no-kami"; in the {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q1052981|lt=Sandai Jitsuroku|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}, as "Noma Tennō-gami"; and in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, as "Noma Shrine." The writing of "Noma" also included characters such as 乃万, 濃満, and 濃万, deriving from {{ill|lt=Noma District|Q266830|WD=Q266830|1=Noma District, Ehime}}（{{ill|lt=Kuni no Miyatsuko Hongi|Q133833194|WD=Q133833194|1=Kokuzo Hongi}}'s {{ill|lt=Nukata Province|Q21654103|WD=Q21654103|lt=Nukata-no-kuni|1=Nukata-no-kuni}}）. In the Kenchō-era documents it appears as "Nomangu," and in Kōfuku-ji's solicited-deity register as "Shōichii Nōman Tennō-gami." There are also examples such as "Daibonten'ō-gū" and "Nomasha Daibonten'ō-gū." Among local people it was called "Tennō-sha" or "Tennō-gū," and popularly known as "Tennon-san." 今治野間神社.jpg|Sanmon（{{ill|lt=2025|Q49616|WD=Q49616|1=2025}}{{ill|lt=December|Q126|WD=Q126|1=December}}） 今治野間神社4.jpg|Hokora（December 2025） 今治野間神社5.jpg|Honden（December 2025） 今治野間神社6.jpg|Stone steps（December 2025） === Cultural properties === ;Important Cultural Property *Stone {{ill|lt=Hōkyōintō|Q3146365|WD=Q3146365|1=Hōkyōintō}} built in {{ill|lt=1322|Q5770|WD=Q5770|1=1322}}（{{ill|lt=Genkō|Q509889|WD=Q509889|1=Genkō (1321–1324)}} 2） ;Prefectural Tangible Cultural Property *11 {{ill|lt=Japanese bronze mirrors|Q1041984|WD=Q1041984|lt=wa-kyō|1=Bronze mirror}} ;Prefectural Intangible Folk Cultural Property *{{ill|lt=Shishimai|Q713866|WD=Q713866|1=Lion dance}} transmitted at Kannomiya Noma Shrine 今治野間神社3.jpg|Important Cultural Property stone {{ill|lt=Hōkyōintō|Q3146365|WD=Q3146365|1=Hōkyōintō}} behind the honden（December 2025） === Annual festival === At the annual grand festival held on May 3, dedicatory performances include the Urayasu dance, daimyo procession, lion dance, mikoshi procession, and hemp-plain sacred boat. Among these, the {{ill|lt=lion dance|Q713866|WD=Q713866|1=Lion dance}} is designated an {{ill|lt=Ehime Prefecture|Q123376|WD=Q123376|1=Ehime Prefecture}} intangible folk cultural property, and the mikoshi procession is designated an {{ill|lt=Imabari|Q461020|WD=Q461020|1=Imabari, Ehime}} intangible folk cultural property. === References === * ''Noma Jinja-shi'', Noma Jinja-shi Editorial Committee（Noma Shrine, 2007） === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Noma Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Noma Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Iyo Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ehime Prefecture]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Iyo Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Ehime Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Imabari, Ehime]] [[Category:History of Imabari, Ehime]] [[Category:Cultural heritage monuments in Japan]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Ehime prefecture]] [[Category:Ehime Prefecture designated tangible cultural property]] [[Category:Prefecturally designated intangible folk cultural property]][[Category:qq]]
